title: Add Finis review path ownership and directory helpers
goal: Extend `azq/finis/storage.py` so Wave A has one shared place to own `data/finis/reviews/` path decisions without changing the canonical goal store.
context: `codex/reports/azq_finis_goal_shaping_gap_report.md` identifies `data/finis/reviews/` as the smallest practical deepening for Stage 4 Wave A.
`azq/finis/storage.py` already owns canonical goal path decisions and is the natural seam for Finis-owned review storage helpers.
The current checked-in `data/finis/` tree contains `goals.json` and `goals/`, but no `reviews/` directory yet.
requirements: Add canonical path constants and directory helpers for `data/finis/reviews/` in `azq/finis/storage.py`.
Add deterministic review-file lookup and stable review-file listing helpers in `azq/finis/storage.py`.
Keep `data/finis/goals/` as the canonical goal home and do not change existing goal path helpers in this task.
Do not introduce `data/finis/proposals/`, `data/finis/questions/`, or `data/finis/intent_packages/` in this task.
acceptance_criteria: `azq/finis/storage.py` owns one explicit shared path seam for `data/finis/reviews/`.
Wave A code can create and list review artifact paths deterministically.
Existing canonical goal path behavior remains intact.
suggested_commit_message: add finis review path helpers for stage 4 wave a

title: Define a minimal Finis review record format and repository helpers
goal: Create one small durable review record contract so Finis can preserve review-boundary state before canonical goal acceptance.
context: The gap report says the missing Stage 4 seam is the durable distinction between raw spark text, candidate goal text, human revision text, and accepted canonical goal text.
`azq/finis/fine.py` currently keeps candidate state only in memory and loses it unless a goal is accepted immediately.
Wave A should add only the first durable review seam, not broader proposal, question, or intent-package artifacts.
requirements: Add a minimal review-record normalizer and read-write helpers in `azq/finis/storage.py`.

The review record must preserve at least: a stable review identifier, exact spark source provenance, raw spark text, candidate goal text, optional human revision text, review status, and accepted canonical goal id when present.
Choose one simple durable on-disk format for review artifacts under `data/finis/reviews/` and make repository reads deterministic.
Do not change the canonical `FINIS_###.md` goal format in this task.
acceptance_criteria: A Finis review record can be normalized, written, listed, and loaded through `azq/finis/storage.py`.
The review format keeps raw spark text and candidate goal text distinct.
No Stage 1 canonical goal helper is replaced or bypassed.
suggested_commit_message: add finis review record read write helpers

title: Make `azq/finis/fine.py` materialize durable review artifacts for candidate goals
goal: Change the live Finis review loop so candidate goals stop existing only as transient terminal state and instead land in visible review artifacts first.
context: `azq/finis/fine.py` currently loads sparks, cleans candidate text, and asks `Accept goal? [y/n]` without persisting pre-goal review state.
The current `load_sparks()` path keeps only `spark` and `source`, so Wave A may need a more exact per-spark review identity than the current in-memory shape provides.
`planning/stage_4/deliverables.json` marks the richer review surface as the first partial Stage 4 deliverable and requires visible review artifacts under `data/finis/reviews/`.
requirements: Update `azq/finis/fine.py` so candidate goal generation writes review artifacts under `data/finis/reviews/` before canonical goal acceptance.
Preserve the current duplicate filtering and used-source filtering behavior unless a small identity adjustment is required to support exact review provenance.
Make sure each review artifact keeps raw spark text and cleaned candidate goal text visibly separate.
Keep `azq fine` as the command entrypoint. Do not add a new public Finis command family in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Running `azq fine` can produce durable Finis review artifacts for candidate goals.
Candidate goal state no longer exists only in transient CLI memory.
Wave A still stays inside the existing Finis engine boundary and command posture.
suggested_commit_message: materialize finis review artifacts from fine candidates

title: Promote accepted Finis reviews into canonical goals through the existing storage seam
goal: Make acceptance happen from durable review state into canonical goal files so the new review seam preserves continuity without redesigning Finis.
context: The gap report's recommended next step is to promote from review artifacts into the existing canonical goal store rather than writing canonical goals directly from transient candidates.
`azq/finis/storage.py` already owns canonical `FINIS_###.md` goal writes, and Wave A should preserve that Stage 1 storage model.
`tests/test_stage1_wave_c.py` already locks the current baseline that accepted goals keep `derived_from` backlinks and land in canonical goal files.
requirements: Update `azq/finis/fine.py` so accepted review records are the source of canonical goal creation rather than transient candidate dicts alone.
When a goal is accepted, write the canonical goal through existing `azq/finis/storage.py` goal helpers and record the acceptance outcome back into the review artifact.

Preserve the current canonical goal behavior for `goal_id`, `status`, `created`, empty `description`, and `derived_from` backlinks unless the review seam requires an additional stable backlink to the accepted review record.
Do not broaden this task into manual goal entry refactors, narrowing questions, or richer review-note fields.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted Finis review state promotes cleanly into `data/finis/goals/FINIS_###.md` through the existing canonical goal storage helpers.
The corresponding review artifact records that acceptance happened and which canonical goal was created.
Wave A preserves the Stage 1 canonical goal storage model while adding a durable pre-goal review boundary.
suggested_commit_message: promote accepted finis reviews into canonical goals

title: Add a narrow Stage 4 regression slice for the Finis review seam
goal: Protect the Wave A deepening with compact regression coverage that proves the new review boundary exists while preserving existing canonical goal behavior.
context: The gap report calls for narrow tests that prove review artifacts exist, preserve provenance boundaries, and still promote into canonical goals through the Stage 1 storage seam.
`tests/test_stage1_wave_c.py` already covers the earlier baseline where `azq fine` writes a canonical goal with a `derived_from` backlink.
Wave A should add only enough regression coverage to lock the new durable review seam without re-testing unrelated Finis behavior.
requirements: Create `tests/test_stage4_wave_a.py` for the new Finis review-seam regression slice.
Add coverage that `azq fine` writes durable review artifacts under `data/finis/reviews/` and keeps raw spark text, candidate goal text, and review status distinct.
Add coverage that accepting a reviewed candidate still writes the canonical goal file under `data/finis/goals/` with the expected Stage 1 fields and `derived_from` backlink.
Add coverage that the accepted review artifact records the canonical goal id it promoted into.
Keep the suite focused on Wave A review-boundary behavior rather than broader Stage 4 aspirations.
acceptance_criteria: The repository has one small Stage 4 Wave A regression file under `tests/`.
Wave A tests prove the new review seam exists and that canonical goal promotion still works through the current Finis storage model.
The new regression slice is safe to run through the repository's existing unittest discovery flow.
suggested_commit_message: add stage 4 wave a finis review seam regression coverage
